NKJV.1: A Psalm. A Song for the Sabbath day. It is good to give thanks to the LORD, And to sing praises to Your name, O Most High;
NKJV.2: To declare Your lovingkindness in the morning, And Your faithfulness every night,
NKJV.3: On an instrument of ten strings, On the lute, And on the harp, With harmonious sound.
NKJV.4: For You, LORD, have made me glad through Your work; I will triumph in the works of Your hands.
NKJV.5: O LORD, how great are Your works!  Your thoughts are very deep.
NKJV.6: A senseless man does not know, Nor does a fool understand this.
NKJV.7: When the wicked spring up like grass, And when all the workers of iniquity flourish, It is that they may be destroyed forever.
NKJV.8: But You, LORD, are on high forevermore.
NKJV.9: For behold, Your enemies, O LORD, For behold, Your enemies shall perish; All the workers of iniquity shall be scattered.
NKJV.10: But my horn You have exalted like a wild ox; I have been anointed with fresh oil.
NKJV.11: My eye also has seen my desire on my enemies; My ears hear my desire on the wicked Who rise up against me.
NKJV.12: The righteous shall flourish like a palm tree, He shall grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
NKJV.13: Those who are planted in the house of the LORD Shall flourish in the courts of our God.
NKJV.14: They shall still bear fruit in old age; They shall be fresh and flourishing,
NKJV.15: To declare that the LORD is upright; He is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in Him.

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> or Song<S>7892</S> for the sabbath<S>7676</S> day<S>3117</S>. It is a good<S>2896</S> thing to give thanks<S>3034</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, and to sing praises<S>2167</S> unto thy name<S>8034</S>, O most High<S>5945</S>:
KJV.2: To shew forth<S>5046</S> thy lovingkindness<S>2617</S> in the morning<S>1242</S>, and thy faithfulness<S>530</S> every night<S>3915</S>, <sup>every: Heb. in the nights</sup>
KJV.3: Upon an instrument of ten strings<S>6218</S>, and upon the psaltery<S>5035</S>; upon the harp<S>3658</S> with a solemn sound<S>1902</S>. <sup>the harp: or, the solemn sound with the harp</sup> <sup>a solemn: Heb. Higgaion</sup>
KJV.4: For thou, LORD<S>3068</S>, hast made me glad<S>8055</S> through thy work<S>6467</S>: I will triumph<S>7442</S> in the works<S>4639</S> of thy hands<S>3027</S>.
KJV.5: O LORD<S>3068</S>, how great<S>1431</S> are thy works<S>4639</S>! and thy thoughts<S>4284</S> are very<S>3966</S> deep<S>6009</S>.
KJV.6: A brutish<S>1198</S> man<S>376</S> knoweth<S>3045</S> not; neither doth a fool<S>3684</S> understand<S>995</S> this.
KJV.7: When the wicked<S>7563</S> spring<S>6524</S> as the grass<S>6212</S>, and when all the workers<S>6466</S> of iniquity<S>205</S> do flourish<S>6692</S>; it is that they shall be destroyed<S>8045</S> for ever<S>5703</S>:
KJV.8: But thou, LORD<S>3068</S>, art most high<S>4791</S> for evermore<S>5769</S>.
KJV.9: For, lo, thine enemies<S>341</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, for, lo, thine enemies<S>341</S> shall perish<S>6</S>; all the workers<S>6466</S> of iniquity<S>205</S> shall be scattered<S>6504</S>.
KJV.10: But my horn<S>7161</S> shalt thou exalt<S>7311</S> like the horn of an unicorn<S>7214</S>: I shall be anointed<S>1101</S> with fresh<S>7488</S> oil<S>8081</S>.
KJV.11: Mine eye<S>5869</S> also shall see<S>5027</S> my desire on mine enemies<S>7790</S>, and mine ears<S>241</S> shall hear<S>8085</S> my desire of the wicked<S>7489</S> that rise up<S>6965</S> against me.
KJV.12: The righteous<S>6662</S> shall flourish<S>6524</S> like the palm tree<S>8558</S>: he shall grow<S>7685</S> like a cedar<S>730</S> in Lebanon<S>3844</S>.
KJV.13: Those that be planted<S>8362</S> in the house<S>1004</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> shall flourish<S>6524</S> in the courts<S>2691</S> of our God<S>430</S>.
KJV.14: They shall still bring forth fruit<S>5107</S> in old age<S>7872</S>; they shall be fat<S>1879</S> and flourishing<S>7488</S>; <sup>flourishing: Heb. green</sup>
KJV.15: To shew<S>5046</S> that the LORD<S>3068</S> is upright<S>3477</S>: he is my rock<S>6697</S>, and there is no unrighteousness<S>5766</S> <S>5766</S> in him.

ESV.1: It is good to give thanks to the LORD, to sing praises to your name, O Most High;
ESV.2: to declare your steadfast love in the morning, and your faithfulness by night,
ESV.3: to the music of the lute and the harp, to the melody of the lyre.
ESV.4: For you, O LORD, have made me glad by your work; at the works of your hands I sing for joy.
ESV.5: How great are your works, O LORD! Your thoughts are very deep!
ESV.6: The stupid man cannot know; the fool cannot understand this:
ESV.7: that though the wicked sprout like grass and all evildoers flourish, they are doomed to destruction forever;
ESV.8: but you, O LORD, are on high forever.
ESV.9: For behold, your enemies, O LORD, for behold, your enemies shall perish; all evildoers shall be scattered.
ESV.10: But you have exalted my horn like that of the wild ox; you have poured over me  fresh oil.
ESV.11: My eyes have seen the downfall of my enemies; my ears have heard the doom of my evil assailants.
ESV.12: The righteous flourish like the palm tree and grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
ESV.13: They are planted in the house of the LORD; they flourish in the courts of our God.
ESV.14: They still bear fruit in old age; they are ever full of sap and green,
ESV.15: to declare that the LORD is upright; he is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in him.

NASB.1: A Psalm, a Song for the Sabbath day. It is good to give thanks to the LORD And to sing praises to Your name, O Most High;
NASB.2: To declare Your lovingkindness in the morning And Your faithfulness by night,
NASB.3: With the ten-stringed lute and with the harp, With resounding music upon the lyre.
NASB.4: For You, O LORD, have made me glad by what You have done, I will sing for joy at the works of Your hands.
NASB.5: How great are Your works, O LORD! Your thoughts are very deep.
NASB.6: A senseless man has no knowledge, Nor does a stupid man understand this:
NASB.7: That when the wicked sprouted up like grass And all who did iniquity flourished, It [was only] that they might be destroyed forevermore.
NASB.8: But You, O LORD, are on high forever.
NASB.9: For, behold, Your enemies, O LORD, For, behold, Your enemies will perish; All who do iniquity will be scattered.
NASB.10: But You have exalted my horn like [that of] the wild ox; I have been anointed with fresh oil.
NASB.11: And my eye has looked [exultantly] upon my foes, My ears hear of the evildoers who rise up against me.
NASB.12: The righteous man will flourish like the palm tree, He will grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
NASB.13: Planted in the house of the LORD, They will flourish in the courts of our God.
NASB.14: They will still yield fruit in old age; They shall be full of sap and very green,
NASB.15: To declare that the LORD is upright; [He is] my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in Him.

NLT.1:
It is good to give thanks to the LORD,
to sing praises to the Most High.
NLT.2:
It is good to proclaim your unfailing love in the morning,
your faithfulness in the evening,
NLT.3:
accompanied by a ten-stringed instrument, a harp,
and the melody of a lyre.
NLT.4:
You thrill me, LORD, with all you have done for me!
I sing for joy because of what you have done.
NLT.5:
O LORD, what great works you do!
And how deep are your thoughts.
NLT.6:
Only a simpleton would not know,
and only a fool would not understand this:
NLT.7:
Though the wicked sprout like weeds
and evildoers flourish,
they will be destroyed forever.
NLT.8: But you, O LORD, will be exalted forever.
NLT.9:
Your enemies, LORD, will surely perish;
all evildoers will be scattered.
NLT.10:
But you have made me as strong as a wild ox.
You have anointed me with the finest oil.
NLT.11:
My eyes have seen the downfall of my enemies;
my ears have heard the defeat of my wicked opponents.
NLT.12:
But the godly will flourish like palm trees
and grow strong like the cedars of Lebanon.
NLT.13:
For they are transplanted to the LORD’s own house.
They flourish in the courts of our God.
NLT.14:
Even in old age they will still produce fruit;
they will remain vital and green.
NLT.15:
They will declare, “The LORD is just!
He is my rock!
There is no evil in him!”

WEB.1: A Psalm. A song for the Sabbath day. It is a good thing to give thanks to Yahweh, to sing praises to your name, Most High;
WEB.2: to proclaim your loving kindness in the morning, and your faithfulness every night,
WEB.3: with the ten-stringed lute, with the harp, and with the melody of the lyre.
WEB.4: For you, Yahweh, have made me glad through your work. I will triumph in the works of your hands.
WEB.5: How great are your works, Yahweh! Your thoughts are very deep.
WEB.6: A senseless man doesn’t know, neither does a fool understand this:
WEB.7: though the wicked spring up as the grass, and all the evildoers flourish, they will be destroyed forever.
WEB.8: But you, Yahweh, are on high forever more.
WEB.9: For, behold, your enemies, Yahweh, for, behold, your enemies shall perish. All the evildoers will be scattered.
WEB.10: But you have exalted my horn like that of the wild ox. I am anointed with fresh oil.
WEB.11: My eye has also seen my enemies. My ears have heard of the wicked enemies who rise up against me.
WEB.12: The righteous shall flourish like the palm tree. He will grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
WEB.13: They are planted in Yahweh’s house. They will flourish in our God’s courts.
WEB.14: They will still produce fruit in old age. They will be full of sap and green,
WEB.15: to show that Yahweh is upright. He is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in him.

AMP.1:
A Psalm. A Song for the Sabbath day. It is a good and delightful thing to give thanks to the LORD,
To sing praises to Your name, O Most High,
AMP.2:
To declare Your lovingkindness in the morning
And Your faithfulness by night,
AMP.3:
With an instrument of ten strings and with the harp,
With a solemn sound on the lyre.
AMP.4:
For You, O LORD, have made me glad by Your works;
At the works of Your hands I joyfully sing.
AMP.5:
How great are Your works, O LORD!
Your thoughts are very deep [beyond man's understanding].
AMP.6:
A senseless man [in his crude and uncultivated state] knows nothing,
Nor does a [self-righteous] fool understand this:
AMP.7:
That though the wicked sprout up like grass
And all evildoers flourish,
They will be destroyed forever.
AMP.8: But You, LORD, are on high forever.
AMP.9:
For behold, Your enemies, O LORD,
For behold, Your enemies will perish;
All who do evil will be scattered.
AMP.10:
But my horn [my emblem of strength and power] You have exalted like that of a wild ox;
I am anointed with fresh oil [for Your service].
AMP.11:
My eye has looked on my foes;
My ears hear of the evildoers who rise up against me.
AMP.12:
The righteous will flourish like the date palm [long-lived, upright and useful];
They will grow like a cedar in Lebanon [majestic and stable].
AMP.13:
Planted in the house of the LORD,
They will flourish in the courts of our God.
AMP.14:
[Growing in grace] they will still thrive and bear fruit and prosper in old age;
They will flourish and be vital and fresh [rich in trust and love and contentment];
AMP.15:
[They are living memorials] to declare that the LORD is upright and faithful [to His promises];
He is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in Him.

MSG.1:
A Sabbath song
What a beautiful thing, God, to give thanks,
to sing an anthem to you, the High God!
MSG.2:
To announce your love each daybreak,
sing your faithful presence all through the night,
MSG.3:
Accompanied by dulcimer and harp,
the full-bodied music of strings.
MSG.4:
You made me so happy, God.
I saw your work and I shouted for joy.
MSG.5:
How magnificent your work, God!
How profound your thoughts!
MSG.6:
Dullards never notice what you do;
fools never do get it.
MSG.7:
When the wicked popped up like weeds
and all the evil men and women took over,
You mowed them down,
finished them off once and for all.
MSG.8: You, God, are High and Eternal.
MSG.9:
Look at your enemies, God!
Look at your enemies—ruined!
Scattered to the winds, all those hirelings of evil!
MSG.10:
But you've made me strong as a charging bison,
you've honored me with a festive parade.
MSG.11:
The sight of my critics going down is still fresh,
the rout of my malicious detractors.
My ears are filled with the sounds of promise:
MSG.12:
"Good people will prosper like palm trees,
Grow tall like Lebanon cedars;
MSG.13:
transplanted to God's courtyard,
They'll grow tall in the presence of God,
MSG.14: lithe and green, virile still in old age."
MSG.15:
Such witnesses to upright God!
My Mountain, my huge, holy Mountain!

NIV.1:
Psalm 92
A psalm. A song. For the Sabbath day.
It is good to praise the Lord
and make music to your name, O Most High,
NIV.2:
to proclaim your love in the morning
and your faithfulness at night,
NIV.3:
to the music of the ten-stringed lyre
and the melody of the harp.
NIV.4:
For you make me glad by your deeds, O Lord;
I sing for joy at the works of your hands.
NIV.5:
How great are your works, O Lord,
how profound your thoughts!
NIV.6:
The senseless man does not know,
fools do not understand,
NIV.7:
that though the wicked spring up like grass
and all evildoers flourish,
they will be forever destroyed.
NIV.8: But you, O Lord, are exalted forever.
NIV.9:
For surely your enemies, O Lord,
surely your enemies will perish;
all evildoers will be scattered.
NIV.10:
You have exalted my horn like that of a wild ox;
fine oils have been poured upon me.
NIV.11:
My eyes have seen the defeat of my adversaries;
my ears have heard the rout of my wicked foes.
NIV.12:
The righteous will flourish like a palm tree,
they will grow like a cedar of Lebanon;
NIV.13:
planted in the house of the Lord,
they will flourish in the courts of our God.
NIV.14:
They will still bear fruit in old age,
they will stay fresh and green,
NIV.15:
proclaiming, “The Lord is upright;
he is my Rock, and there is no wickedness in him.”

NRSVCE.1:
It is good to give thanks to the LORD,
to sing praises to your name, O Most High;
NRSVCE.2:
to declare your steadfast love in the morning,
and your faithfulness by night,
NRSVCE.3:
to the music of the lute and the harp,
to the melody of the lyre.
NRSVCE.4:
For you, O LORD, have made me glad by your work;
at the works of your hands I sing for joy.
NRSVCE.5:
How great are your works, O LORD!
Your thoughts are very deep!
NRSVCE.6:
The dullard cannot know,
the stupid cannot understand this:
NRSVCE.7:
though the wicked sprout like grass
and all evildoers flourish,
they are doomed to destruction forever,
NRSVCE.8: but you, O LORD, are on high forever.
NRSVCE.9:
For your enemies, O LORD,
for your enemies shall perish;
all evildoers shall be scattered.
NRSVCE.10:
But you have exalted my horn like that of the wild ox;
you have poured over me fresh oil.
NRSVCE.11:
My eyes have seen the downfall of my enemies;
my ears have heard the doom of my evil assailants.
NRSVCE.12:
The righteous flourish like the palm tree,
and grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
NRSVCE.13:
They are planted in the house of the LORD;
they flourish in the courts of our God.
NRSVCE.14:
In old age they still produce fruit;
they are always green and full of sap,
NRSVCE.15:
showing that the LORD is upright;
he is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in him.

NET.1: It is fitting to thank the LORD, and to sing praises to your name, O sovereign One!
NET.2: It is fitting to proclaim your loyal love in the morning, and your faithfulness during the night,
NET.3: to the accompaniment of a ten-stringed instrument and a lyre, to the accompaniment of the meditative tone of the harp.
NET.4: For you, O LORD, have made me happy by your work. I will sing for joy because of what you have done.
NET.5: How great are your works, O LORD! Your plans are very intricate!
NET.6: The spiritually insensitive do not recognize this; the fool does not understand this.
NET.7: When the wicked sprout up like grass, and all the evildoers glisten, it is so that they may be annihilated.
NET.8: But you, O LORD, reign forever!
NET.9: Indeed, look at your enemies, O LORD! Indeed, look at how your enemies perish! All the evildoers are scattered!
NET.10: You exalt my horn like that of a wild ox. I am covered with fresh oil.
NET.11: I gloat in triumph over those who tried to ambush me; I hear the defeated cries of the evil foes who attacked me.
NET.12: The godly grow like a palm tree; they grow high like a cedar in Lebanon.
NET.13: Planted in the LORD’s house, they grow in the courts of our God.
NET.14: They bear fruit even when they are old; they are filled with vitality and have many leaves.
NET.15: So they proclaim that the LORD, my protector, is just and never unfair.
